title: Time-Series Transformers, Patched
tagline: Why PatchTST cuts a sequence into ViT-style patches and finally beats LSTMs
summary: Long-horizon forecasting on an air-quality–style signal, side-by-side: seasonal-naive baseline, ARIMA, a small LSTM, and PatchTST. Slide the patch length, watch the forecast change. The article closes with where state-space models (Mamba) fit and what's next.
highlights: Synthetic AQ-style signal (daily + weekly + noise + drift) generated live; baseline, ARIMA-flavour, LSTM-flavour and PatchTST-flavour forecasts overlay.
Slide patch length and horizon and watch RMSE update across the four models.
Patch-tokenisation diagram with positional encoding so the transformer side is concrete.
Lab-aligned: AirDelhi mobile sensors, JoulesEye / ApneaEye breathing rate, energy disaggregation.
